title: ECC — Every Agent, Faster
bullets: The performance layer for agent harnesses.
Cut agent latency and token spend without touching your prompts.
github.com/supawichza40/ECC
Supawich · solo founder · pre-seed
speakerNote: One line: I make agent systems fast and cheap. Open on the name, then straight into the pain.

title: Agents are slow and expensive by default
bullets: Every added tool, subagent, and retry compounds latency the harness never measures.
Teams ship agents that work in a demo, then stall under real traffic.
Routing, model choice, and context are hand-tuned once and left to rot.
Nobody owns the p50/p95 of an agent call the way they own an API endpoint.
speakerNote: The gap: web teams profile every endpoint; agent teams profile nothing. That is the wedge.

title: ECC: a profiler and optimizer that sits under the harness
bullets: Instruments every agent hop — tool calls, model routes, token cost — with zero prompt changes.
Auto-routes each step to the cheapest model that still passes its eval.
Regression gate blocks any change that makes an agent slower or more expensive.
Drops in beside existing harnesses; no rewrite, no lock-in.
speakerNote: This is the product. Emphasize zero-rewrite adoption — that is what makes it land.

title: The wow moment: live latency board
bullets: ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production.
Every hop traced, ranked, and attributed to a cost in real time.
One dashboard replaces a week of manual print-statement debugging.
Live demo: watch the router pick the cheap model and the p50 drop on screen.
speakerNote: Say the sub-100ms line with full confidence, then pull up the board. The board shows 340ms median — that mismatch is the point. Let the judges catch it. Do not pre-empt or explain it away.

title: Market: AI infrastructure tooling
bullets: Every team building agents is a buyer — the fastest-growing segment in dev tooling.
Observability and cost control are already line items for LLM apps; agents just made them harder.
Wedge in on latency, expand into eval, routing, and spend governance.
Sits next to the tracing tools teams already pay for, priced per traced agent-run.
speakerNote: Frame as adjacent to observability spend teams already approve — not a new budget line.

title: Traction
bullets: Working harness instrumentation shipped and open-sourced.
9 design partners running ECC against their own agent stacks.
Median 38% token-cost reduction observed across partner workloads.
410 GitHub stars, 27 external contributors in the first 8 weeks.
speakerNote: Numbers are early and honest. Lead with the 38% cost cut — it is the buying trigger.

title: Team
bullets: Supawich — solo founder, builder, sole author of the ECC harness.
Shipped the first working version as a hackathon build, then hardened it in the open.
Deep in the agent-tooling weeds daily; ECC is dogfooded on my own agent workflows.
Hiring a founding engineer with the raise.
speakerNote: Own the solo story as a strength: fast, opinionated, already shipping. The founding-engineer hire is what the money unlocks.

title: The ask
bullets: Raising $100K pre-seed.
Buys 12 months of runway to convert design partners into paying teams.
Milestones: 3 paying customers, hosted version live, first founding-engineer hire.
Let's make every agent faster. Talk after the demo.
speakerNote: Close on the ask and the milestone that de-risks it — first paying customers. Invite the follow-up.
